name: Veal-And-Crayfish Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. COMBINE the veal and 4 cups broth in a 5-quart heavy pot over high heat on top of the stove. Cover and bring to a boil.
2. Remove any scum that comes to the top of the pot, then add the remaining broth, onion, celery, carrot, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and lemon. Reduce heat to low and let simmer for 1 1/4 hours, or until meat is just tender.
3. Meanwhile, MELT the butter in a pot over low heat, whisk in the flour. Cook, stirring, for 2 minutes and remove from heat. SET ASIDE.
4. REMOVE the meat from the liquid with a slotted spoon. PICK OFF AND DISCARD any vegetables that cling to the veal.
5. PLACE the butter-flour mixture over medium-low heat on the stove. STRAIN the cooking liquid into it, whisking, and add the sherry and cream. DISCARD VEGETABLES IN THE STRAINER.
6. COOK, stirring until the mixture thickens, about 3 to 5 minutes.
7. REPLACE the meat in the pot and ADD the mushrooms and crayfish. POUR IN the sauce, place the pot uncovered over medium heat, and cook for 10 minutes.
8. SERVE into a serving dish.
